verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone retained by its KDEL motif with no intrinsic TM domain or GPI anchor. Under ER stress conditions — including hypoxia, nutrient deprivation, oncogenic transformation, and viral infection — a subfraction escapes ER retention and translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. This cell-surface HSPA5 (csGRP78) pool is well-documented across multiple cancer types (breast, prostate, pancreas, lung, ovarian, melanoma) where it functions as a co-receptor and signaling hub (PI3K/AKT), is targeted by therapeutic antibodies and peptides (Pep42), and serves as a SARS-CoV-2 entry receptor. Surface display is largely absent on normal, unstressed cells. This is a prototypic cancer/stress-state ecto-presentation.
